title: 10 possible side effects of Schisandra chinensis (Schisandra) if used improperly
intro: Schisandra chinensis, a plant commonly used in traditional medicine, can have negative health consequences if used improperly. Overconsumption may lead to digestive issues such as stomach pain, diarrhea, and decreased appetite. Additionally, it can cause skin rashes and irritations, as well as respiratory problems in some individuals. In extreme cases, prolonged use or high doses may result in more severe side effects, including liver toxicity. Pregnant women and those with kidney or liver disorders should avoid using Schisandra chinensis altogether, as it may exacerbate existing conditions. Always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen.

side_effects.side_effect_name: Inflammation of the mouth and tongue
